scope_note: QA Langlands Sym2 Full GL6/Q Euler Factor cert [408]. Primary sources: Shimura, G. (1975) 'On the holomorphy of certain Dirichlet series' Proceedings of the London Mathematical Society 31 pp.79-98 doi:10.1007/BF01403156 (symmetric square L-functions for GL_2); Gelbart, S. and Jacquet, H. (1978) 'A relation between automorphic representations of GL(2) and GL(3)' Annales scientifiques de l'ENS 11 pp.471-542 doi:10.2307/1971237 (Sym^2 lift GL_2 to GL_3); Kim, H.H. and Shahidi, F. (2002) doi:10.4007/annals.2002.155.837 (functorial product, Sym^3 for GL_2); Cogdell, J.W. (2004) 'L-functions and Converse Theorems for GL_n' ISBN 978-0-8218-3516-0 (GL_n automorphic L-functions and functional equations). CLAIM (narrow and falsifiable): For f=2.2.5.1-125.1-a and split prime p≡1(mod5), the full Sym^2 automorphic L-function at p has Euler factor V_p(Y)=(1-pY)^2*Sigma_p(Y) where Sigma_p is the degree-4 reduced factor from cert [407]. Expanded: V_p is a degree-6 integer polynomial obtained by convolution V_p[k]=Sigma_p[k]-2p*Sigma_p[k-1]+p^2*Sigma_p[k-2]. Four checks: (C1) all 7 integer coefficients in Z (22/22 PASS); (C2) weight-2 palindrome a6=p^6, a5=p^4*a1, a4=p^2*a2 (22/22 PASS); (C3) V_p=(1-pY)^2*Sigma_p verified by two independent convolution paths (22/22 PASS); (C4) GL6 Ramanujan: (1-pY)^2 roots at Y=1/p, quadratic factors of Sigma_p have disc<0 from cert [403] Ramanujan; all 6 roots have |root|=p^{-1} (22/22 PASS). Theorem NT: cᵢ=sigma_i(a_p)^2-2p are observer projections only. Ladder: [403]->[404]->[407]->[408]. Parallel branch: [405]->[406] (exterior square). Derived 2026-06-13.
